items.speaker: Olof
items.text: Just as expected. I had no hope of winning against you.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: But... thank you. For not holding back. For not giving me a pity victory.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: To me, pity cuts deeper than any loss. That's the real reason I couldn't face the club.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: I kept seeing sympathy, pity, and worse... a sense of mocking in everyone's eyes. I know they didn't mean it that way, but I just couldn't shake it.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: I know, otherwise they wouldn't have asked you to come here. But as you can see... I still need time to think about why I want to keep dueling.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: The last time I was at the fan club, club leader Anita asked me a question.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: "What's the point of being a duelist if you're so fixated on winning that you forget the thrill of the duel itself?"

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: Heh. I still don't have an answer for her.

items.speaker: Olof
items.text: Don't worry, {PlayerName}. I won't give up that easily. I'll find some time to go back and see everyone so they'll stop worrying.
